---
title: "Traceable chart authoring with ggaction"
subtitle: "A Quarto and Observable JS example"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: show
    css: styles.css
execute:
  echo: true
---

This document uses Quarto's reactive Observable JS runtime and the exact public
`ggaction` 0.0.7 package. The chart is authored with ggaction's public chained
actions; there is no intermediate chart grammar or translated specification.

```{ojs}
import {
  GGActionVersion,
  buildProgram,
  createChartFigure,
  createTraceInspector
} from "./ggaction-ojs.js"
```

Quarto exposes `width` as a reactive value. When the document layout changes,
the program is rebuilt immutably for the available width and the Canvas output
is rendered again.

```{ojs}
responsiveProgram = buildProgram(width)
```

## Chart

```{ojs}
//| label: fig-ggaction-cars
//| echo: false
createChartFigure(responsiveProgram)
```

The figure includes a concise text alternative and an expandable data table.
The Canvas renderer uses at most a 2× pixel ratio to stay sharp without
allocating an unnecessarily large backing bitmap.

## Action trace inspection

The inspector reads `responsiveProgram.trace` directly. Expand any node to see
the wrapped actions ggaction retained beneath each authored action.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
createTraceInspector(responsiveProgram)
```

## Version

```{ojs}
md`This page imports public ggaction ${GGActionVersion} from jsDelivr.`
```
